#Summary1=(human1) In the court case SJC 13721, Town of Concord versus Neil Rasmussen and others, Attorney Gwen King, on behalf of the appellants, addresses the question of how a way becomes public and also how a public way is discontinued and ceases to be public, while Justice Kafka and other judges ask clarifying questions.

#Summary2=(claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929; postedited) Attorney King, representing appellants in a dispute over whether a road remains public, argues that the 1932 discontinuance by Middlesex County Commissioners under section 32A could legally end the public status of ways, distinguishing this from section 21 town discontinuances, and questioning whether the way could have remained public through lost layout or prescription.

#Summary3=(gpt4o; postedited) Attorney Gwen King argues before Justices of the Court that the key issues in Town of Concord versus Neil Rasmussen involve how a way becomes public, how it is discontinued, and the legal distinctions between the relevant statutory sections, with emphasis on the public's access rights and the authority of county commissioners.

#Summary4=(Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct; postedited) Attorney Gwen King argues whether the 1932 discontinuance of a public way by the Middlesex County Commissioners under the 1924 act rendered it private.

#Summary5=(Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct; postedited) Attorney Gwen King argues before the Court whether the 1932 discontinuance by the Middlesex County Commissioners under the 1924 act legally discontinues a public way, and whether a way can be deemed public due to a lost layout, challenging the current interpretation of public way discontinuance laws.
#Text=SJC 13721 .
#Text=Town of Concord versus Neil Rasmussen and others .
#Text=Okay , Attorney King , whenever you 're ready .
#Text=All set .
#Text=Good morning .
#Text=May it please the Court .
#Text=Gwen King , on behalf of the appellants .
#Text=Before the Court today is a question of both how a way becomes public and also how a public way is discontinued and ceases to be public .
#Text=During my time , I 'd like to address three issues for the Court .
#Text=First , the effect of the 1932 discontinuance by the Middlesex County Commissioners under the 1924 act to discontinue public ways .

#Text=Second , whether a way can be deemed public by virtue of a lost layout where there is no evidence regarding when it was laid out , who it was laid out by , and most importantly , under what authority it was laid out .
#Text=And third , whether the Court should eliminate the high bar of establishing a public way by prescription , where the claim of public prescription is an ancient claim .
#Text=First , as to the effect of the 1932 discontinuance , a fundamental rule of land law is that a way is public until it is -- until it is legally discontinued .
#Text=Well , what is a legally -- a legal discontinuance ?
#Text=It is a discontinuance according to law .
#Text=In 1924 , the legislature —
#Text=Just to be more specific .
#Text=There are three different ways , right ?
#Text=There 's 32A .
#Text=There 's 21 and 30 .
#Text=Right ?
#Text=Correct .
#Text=Are n't you sort of saying all three work exactly the same ?
#Text=I 'm -- that 's -- I 'm a little confused by your argument that they 're not doing different things .
#Text=Well , they definitely have different procedures and in some instances different outcomes .
#Text=So section 21 , let 's take that first .
#Text=Just just pin you down and I 'll let you talk .
#Text=But my understanding is that your view all three of those turn it into a purely private way .
#Text=Right ?
#Text=Well I -- with a slight distinction , Your Honor .
#Text=So under Nylander , this Court held a section 21 discontinuance , which is a discontinuance at town meeting of a town way .
#Text=And only a town way under section 21 does n't leave any abutter access rights .
#Text=There is no private way left intact after section 21 discontinuance per this court in Nylander .
#Text=However —
#Text=I thought the footnote in Nylander is the big , foundation -- is the foundation of their argument for why this remains still public .
#Text=So the footnote that 's emphasized by the town is referring to a subsequent version of 32A .
#Text=And in that this court noted that that leaves intact public access rights .
#Text=Now , we have contended in our brief that what the court was construing is public access rights is distinct from how the town characterized it .
#Text=Most notably , as you can see in the underlying appeals court and superior court decision in Nylander public access , private way was referring to abutters ' private rights of access .
#Text=And I emphasized that as an important point because , as we note , the public right to travel is the public easement that is laid out under chapter 82 .
#Text=The public right of access is is not something in this court .
#Text=In the 2002 case , Chandler actually noted the distinction and said , you ca n't lay out a way for access .

#Text=You can onl- -- the public right is only that of travel , and that 's inconsistent with what is clearly the legislature 's intent in the 1924 act , because in the 1924 act , they say it 's no longer needed for common convenience and necessity , and it 's not needed to be safe and convenient for travel .
#Text=That 's inconsistent with the concept of what a layout for a public way is needed to be for , to be safe and convenient for travel .
#Text=The inverse decision was adjudicated by the county commissioners here , and the adjudication is a very important point as to the distinct meaning of section 32A .
#Text=Can you ?
#Text=I have , I think , a somewhat related question to Justice Kafka 's question .
#Text=If we look at section 21 , as it was in effect in 1932 , and section 32A as it was in effect at that time , uh , what is the difference in outcome ?
#Text=Are you saying that it -- that action under either section would result in exactly the same outcome ?
#Text=No , I 'm not .
#Text=And the question actually shows why section 32A was so important to municipalities and the Commonwealth at the time .
#Text=So first , as to the jurisdiction of section 21 , that only applies to town ways .
#Text=However , the jurisdiction of 32A applies to public ways which the county commissioners had jurisdiction over all public ways at the time .
#Text=And it 's important to go back in time to 1924 when thinking about the jurisdiction of the county commissioners .
#Text=They had authority over public ways being county highways , town ways , and any public way of unknown jurisdiction .
#Text=So that 's an important distinction because here where , as made clear by the the case before you , what this way was was at minimum uncertain .
#Text=So the county commissioners would have the authority to discontinue it under 32A , whereas the town would not have authority to discontinue a county way under section 21 .
#Text=I did n't see anything to suggest this was a county way .
#Text=Well , to the contrary , the town contends that there was a 1763 county layout of the Northern Trail that was effectuated by the county commissioners .
#Text=We have disputed the -- whether that was effective because the conditions -- precedents -- precedents were not fulfilled .
#Text=The town notes in their brief that -- and relies and emphasizes that certain deeds of the Northern Trail said -- referred to the way as a county way .
